# manager.trainer.get_last_model { #spotforecast2_safe.manager.trainer.get_last_model }

```python
manager.trainer.get_last_model(model_name, model_dir=None)
```

Get the latest trained model from the cache.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type                                                                                             | Description                                                                       | Default    |
|------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| model_name | [str](`str`)                                                                                     | Name of the model (e.g., 'lgbm', 'xgb').                                          | _required_ |
| model_dir  | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[Union](`typing.Union`)\[[str](`str`), [Path](`pathlib.Path`)\]\] | Directory where models are stored. If None, defaults to the library's cache home. | `None`     |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                | Description                                                |
|--------|---------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [int](`int`)        | A tuple (iteration, model_instance). If no model is found, |
|        | [Any](`typing.Any`) | returns (-1, None).                                        |
